evals.expected_output: The assistant checks Databricks CLI auth, runs the sql_dependency_analyzer.py script with --top 20 --row-counts, and produces a full Markdown report with Executive Summary, Dependency Hotspots table, Performance Findings per table, and a Recommendations Summary.
expectations: The assistant checks CLI installation and authentication before running the script
The script is invoked with --top 20 and --row-counts flags
The Markdown report includes an Executive Summary with graph stats
The report includes a Dependency Hotspots table sorted by impact_score
At least one Performance Findings sub-section with detected issues is present

evals.expected_output: The assistant runs the analyzer, produces the report, then creates the git branch perf/march-fixes, applies fixes to the top-10 tables (SELECT DISTINCT → GROUP BY ALL, NOT IN → LEFT ANTI JOIN, etc.), runs sqlfmt after each edit, commits, and appends an Applied Fixes section to the report.
expectations: The assistant creates a git branch named perf/march-fixes
SQL rewrites are applied: at least one of SELECT DISTINCT, NOT IN subquery, or Repeated scan fixes
sqlfmt is run after each file edit
A git commit is made with a descriptive message referencing the tables fixed
An Applied Fixes section is appended to the report

evals.expected_output: The assistant runs the script with --graph and --graph-output flags (no --fix), then explains what the interactive HTML graph shows: node colors (red/orange/blue by in-degree tercile), node sizes (proportional to dependents), and directed edges.
expectations: The script is run with --graph and --graph-output /tmp/dep_graph.html
The assistant explains node color coding (red = high in-degree, orange = mid, blue = low)
The assistant mentions node sizes are proportional to dependency count
The output HTML file path is confirmed to the user
